9mo ago

Image 19: KBX

KBX

•1 review

Really impressed by how easy it is to create 3D models with Tripo. I used it to build Chelsea’s first-team squad in 3D — and even though Madueke just moved to Arsenal, I still have his Chelsea version saved, which feels special. The tool is simple, fast, and surprisingly fun. Highly recommend it for anyone who wants to capture and preserve moments in 3D.

#### What's great

fast performance (3)fun to use (2)ease of use (5)3D model creation (8)

### Q: Are browser-based motion design tools able to export 4K video and GIFs?

Jitter is praised for very fast motion-file exports, but the review doesn't state whether it can export 4K video or GIFs. There’s no explicit mention in these notes that any browser tool here exports 4K or GIFs directly. Two useful points to consider:

If you need 4K/GIF support, check each product’s export settings, try a free export, or ask support.

results.title: Meshy Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Patrick Heys

#### What's great

It is exactly what it claims to be. Works easy and while not intuitive makes sense one you undertand the basics.

#### What needs improvement

Cleaner tutorials but even that is being very picky. I really have no complaints

#### vs Alternatives

Easy of use and they coin usage system.

#### What's great

This is the best 3D model platform i have ever used, and trust me i have test many, meshy is my go to when ever i need a model, though i tell hope they work more on 2D to 3D for little bugs it has, i recommend this platform to anyone, looking to create a model, whether from text, images or 2D meshy is for you

model generation with different components, like model faces [...] #### vs Alternatives

cause of the fast 3D generations, excellent model

Hannah

Meshy

Ethan (Yuanming) Hu

Meshy [...] This app took my Blender projects and Snapchat Filter creations to a higher level almost overnight and it did this without forcing me to relinquish any control in the process. In 2 months of using it daily I can say that Meshy doesn't make any compromises in regards to 3D generation speed in relation to the integrity of the mesh output. I use 'integrity' instead of 'quality' here because, for example, in photo-to-3D if the 'quality' is not deemed as 'high' it is a subjective designation, which means it is not what you were hoping for, but because of generation speed and ease of use, you can make a quick edit of the image and try again with such speed and ease that you rapidly gain trust that you can always get what you envisioned even if you disregarded all other features and solely used
